matches.note: Lamine Yamal and a Mikel Oyarzabal brace inside the first 24 minutes put Spain in command, and a Hassan Al-Tambakti own goal early in the second half completed a 4-0 win over Saudi Arabia in Atlanta.
Cape Verde twice fought back to earn a 2-2 draw with two-time champions Uruguay in Miami, Helio Varela's 61st-minute strike capping a resilient display that continued the Blue Sharks' underdog run.

Finn Surman headed New Zealand in front, but Egypt roared back after half-time through Mostafa Ziko, Mohamed Salah and Trezeguet to win 3-1 in Vancouver for the Pharaohs' first-ever World Cup victory.
